text:
In a quaint little village nestled among rolling hills, there lived a wise old tortoise named Percival. Percival was renowned for his patience and wisdom, which often made him the center of various woodland tales. One misty morning, while collecting dewdrops to refresh the flowers with his morning tea, Percival heard a faint chirping.

Curious, he moved slowly toward the source. Along the way, he met a cheerful chipmunk. "Good morning, Percival!" the chipmunk exclaimed. Percival tipped his shell slightly. "Good morning to you, my dear," he replied, "what's chirping so joyously?"

The chipmunk hopping around stopped and sighed, "Oh Percival, it's this acorn hoard. The squirrels took all mine last week!"

Percival nodded sympathetically. "I understand. Have you any wisdom to share today?"

With a bounce, the chipmunk leaned forward eagerly. "Wisdom? Of course
